channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: ICE in Boston nabs previously deported Dominican fentanyl trafficker, child rape convict, murderers
item.description: ICE agents in the Boston area have arrested previously deported fentanyl migrant traffickers, a child rape convict and several migrants wanted for murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinians chanted against Hamas during anti-war protests in the Gaza Strip, witnesses said Wednesday, in a rare show of public anger against the militant group that has long repressed dissent and still rules the territory 17 months into the war with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: German authorities raid group that allegedly aims to overthrow Eritrean government
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Police were executing raids in six German states Wednesday against a group that authorities say “is part of an international network whose aim is to overthrow the government in Eritrea.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Perpetual adoration is a growing Catholic trend. A Holy Year event is scheduled for the weekend
item.description: Growing numbers of Catholics are taking part in Eucharistic adoration – which this year’s Jubilee will mark this weekend with “24 hours for the Lord.”.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KRON4

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene fueled wildfires in North Carolina and South Carolina, where evacuation orders were in effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pilot and 2 young daughters survive the night on airplane wing after crashing into icy Alaska lake
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot and his two young daughters survived on the wing of a plane for about 12 hours after it crashed and was partially submerged in an icy Alaska lake, then were rescued after being spotted by a good Samaritan.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate confirms Michael Kratsios to lead White House science, tech office
item.description: Kratsios, who was Trump’s chief technology officer during his first White House term, was approved Tuesday by the upper chamber in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Columbia student protester can't be detained for now as she fights deportation, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Columbia University student from South Korea who is facing potential deportation for her involvement in a pro-Palestinian protest can't be taken into immigration detention for now, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a trip to Nashville, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Wealthy coastal enclave nanny charged with manslaughter in toddler's death
item.description: Aimee Cotton, a babysitter on Martha's Vineyard, allegedly left small children unattended in her vehicle for three hours, leading to the death of one child.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transgender Americans aim to block Trump's passport policy change
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When Ash Lazarus Orr went to renew his passport in early January, the transgender organizer figured it would be relatively routine. But more than two months on, Orr is waiting to get a new passport with a name change and a sex designation reflecting who he is.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Boston skating community hosts the world championships even as it mourns plane crash victims
item.description: NORWOOD, Mass. (AP) — The chairs where Jinna Han and Spencer Lane used to sit are still there, and covered with gifts: cards and cookies, plants and pictures — anything that might have sparked a connection for their friends and fellow skaters.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More states move to ban cellphones in school as Georgia is latest to act
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — More states are moving to ban or restrict cellphones in school, a trend that continues to win bipartisan support in legislatures.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Atlanta airport reveals mural of President Jimmy and Roselyn Carter
item.description: A new mural paying tribute to President Jimmy Carter and his wife Roselyn is greeting travelers at Atlanta's airport.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI launches Tesla vandalism task force
item.description: (The Hill) -- The FBI confirmed Monday it has established a task force to investigate the wave of vandalism and arson incidents targeting Tesla in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Michigan couple held in Mexican prison, daughter says
item.description: They couple was arrested on March 4 at an airport in Cancun and have been held without bond for 21 days, the daughter claims.
item.dc:creator: Taryn Simmons, KRON4

item.title: US infrastructure improved with Biden-era spending but there's a long way to go
item.description: A report card on the upkeep of America's infrastructure gave it a “C” grade on Tuesday, up slightly from previous years.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Andrew Tate, brother Tristan leave Florida to return to Romanian police station in trafficking case
item.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared at a Romanian police station on Monday to comply with a court-mandated summons as they face numerous sex crimes charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Popular rock band's drummer responds after getting fired over 'unfortunate' conduct
item.description: The popular jam band Goose has announced the departure of its drummer over alleged personal "behavior" issues.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: You're an American in another land? Prepare to talk about the why and how of Trump 2.0
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The urgent care doctor cocked an eyebrow at Mari Santos and her American accent. It was four days after President Donald Trump's inauguration, and Santos was a student with a stomach bug in the first weeks of an overseas semester in Glasgow, Scotland.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Trump-Johnson alliance is working. For now
item.description: The President and House speaker have a relationship built on trust, loyalty and results, sources tell NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will nominate acting CDC director Susan Monarez for the position, White House official says
item.description: President Donald Trump will nominate Susan Monarez, the acting director of the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and a longtime federal staffer, to the permanent position, a White House official confirmed Monday.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, AMANDA SEITZ and MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to halt judge's order to rehire probationary federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Monday to halt a ruling ordering the rehiring of thousands of federal workers let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kidnapped 6-year-old girl discovered during traffic stop: Kansas troopers
item.description: A trooper's discovery during a routine traffic stop last month led to the rescue of a 6-year-old girl who had been kidnapped for weeks, according to the Kansas Highway Patrol.
item.dc:creator: Barry Owens, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks three agencies from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday indefinitely blocked the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies, the latest roadblock to its efforts to slim down the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Atlantic journalist says Trump officials included him in war plans group chat
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, editor in chief of The Atlantic, was apparently included in a Trump administration group chat on Signal in which top officials debated and then discussed details of attacks against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Popular rock band's drummer fires back after getting dismissed
item.description: The popular jam band Goose has announced the departure of its drummer over alleged personal "behavior" issues. Now, he's firing back.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Maps: The states, counties with the most excessive drinkers, per new report
item.description: "Excessive drinking" includes binge drinking, heavy drinking, drinking while pregnant, and underage drinking, according to the CDC.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Florida ride-share driver thwarts $8K cereal box scam
item.description: OKALOOSA COUNTY, Fla. (WKRG) — A ride-share driver who became suspicious of a job ended up saving a woman from becoming the victim of a scam, according to the Okaloosa County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Summer Poole, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tax season is a prime time for scams. IRS uncertainty could add to the issues this year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a lot of information (and money) on the table during tax season. That also makes it a prime time for scams. Year-round, fraudsters may use a handful of common tactics to try to steal your identity, money or other sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tiger Woods confirms relationship with Vanessa Trump
item.description: “Love is in the air and life is better with you by my side! We look forward to our journey through life together,” Woods said on X, where he has 6.4 million followers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope returns to Vatican after 5-week hospital stay over life-threatening bout of pneumonia
item.description: A weak and frail Pope Francis returned home to the Vatican from the hospital on Sunday after surviving a five-week, life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What causes the powerful winds that fuel dust storms, wildfires and blizzards? A weather scientist explains
item.description: Windstorms can seem like they come out of nowhere, hitting with a sudden blast. They might be hundreds of miles long, stretching over several states, or just in your neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Chris Nowotarski Associate Professor of Atmospheric Science, Texas A&M University, Fox 5

item.title: Benadryl bottles recalled over risk of child poisoning
item.description: More than 2,000 bottles of liquid Benadryl have been recalled due to a packaging issue, according to a notice posted by the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9
